title: Start index should be preferred instead of calling Substring
description: Start index should be used instead of calling SubstringAnalyze your codeintentionality-efficientmaintainabilityCode SmellperformanceWhy is this an issue?More InfoIt is important to be careful when searching for characters within a substring. Let’s consider the following example:if (str.SubString(startIndex).IndexOf(char1) == -1) // Noncompliant: a new string is going to be created by "Substring" { // ... }A newstringobject is created with every call to theSubstringmethod.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Use IndexOf with startIndex parameter instead of Substring().IndexOf() to avoid creating unnecessary string objects.

logic.reasoning: Substring creates a new string object on each call. Using IndexOf with startIndex avoids this allocation.
